page: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor of the ARMv8 architecture, operating at a sustained clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This low-memory ratio positions the instance within a compute-focused tier. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the D16pls_v5 in the middle tier for most multi-threaded and memory-bound operations. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28902.52 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9624.0. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1127.02 GB/sec, dropping to 126.1 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the bottom 25 percent of benchmarks. Cryptographic performance is also relatively weak, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU encryption score of 2592.32, despite hardware AES support.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16pls_v5 offers a high physical core density on ARM architecture, making it a viable option for scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores rather than hyperthreads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-oriented tasks with low memory footprints, such as static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and Redis database operations. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases, heavy cryptographic processing, or workloads requiring high single-core throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the DPLSv5 family, utilizing 16 dedicated physical cores of an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, representing a low-memory 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs but is premium storage capable. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28902.52 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9624.0. However, single-core performance and cryptographic operations represent key tradeoffs, scoring in the bottom 25 percent of benchmarks. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for core-dense, multi-threaded workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as static web serving, software compilation, and Redis operations, while being less suited for memory-heavy or single-thread-bound applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for multi-threaded, low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16pls_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Ampere cores and 32 GB of RAM, designed for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-focused instance featuring sixteen dedicated physical cores and a low-memory profile for efficient multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores clocked at 3.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
No bundled local storage or GPUs, with premium storage capability
Strong multi-threaded performance with average memory bandwidth
Tradeoffs include weaker single-core and cryptographic benchmark results
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis operations
